sections.paragraph:
καθηλόω 1 aor. καθήλωσα; pf. pass. ptc. καθηλωμένος (κατά + ἧλος ‘nail’; Polyb. 1, 22, 5; Diod S 20, 85, 2; Hero I 442, 10; Plut., Alex. 24, 7; SIG 969, 57; 84; PLond IV, 1384, 41; LXX) to fasten w. nails, nail on, of one condemned to be burned at the stake MPol 14:1 (cp. Ps.-Callisth. 2, 18, 2 ἐδέδεντο ἐν πέδαις καθηλωταῖς). Of Christ on the cross: τὰς σάρκας B 5:13 (Ps 118:120); ISm 1:2. Hence of Christians ὥσπερ καθηλωμένοι ἐν τῷ σταυρῷ τοῦ κυρίου Ἰ. Χρ. σαρκί τε καὶ πνεύματι as if nailed to the cross of the Lord Jesus Christ in body and in spirit 1:1.—DELG s.v. ἧλος.

aor. aor. = aorist

  1. pf. = perfect

pass. pass. = passive (either of grammatical form or of passive experience); also used in reference to literary portion=passage

ptc. ptc. = participle

Polyb Polyb , III–II b.c.—List 5

Diod S Diod S , I b.c.—List 5

Plut Plut , I–II a.d.—List 5

SIG SIG = Sylloge Inscriptionum Graecarum3; superscript omitted in text—List 3

PLond PLond = PLondon=Greek Papyri in the British Museum—List 4

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

MPol MPol = Martyrdom of Polycarp; after II a.d.—List 1

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

Ps.-Callisth Ps.-Callisth , Historia Alexandri Magni, II–III? a.d.—Lists 5

B B = Barnabas (the Letter of), II a.d., except in series of uncial witnesses, in which case B refers to Codex Vaticanus (s. also Vat.). When the abbrv. B would ambiguous, Vat. is used for the codex.—List 1

ISm ISm = Ignatius to the Smyrnaeans, I–II a.d.—List 1

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 491
